subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Components
- **Character cards** — Loyal Servants of Arthur (Good), Minions of Mordred (Evil), Merlin (Good), Assassin (Evil).
- **Optional character cards** — Percival, Morgana, Oberon, Mordred (each adds a layer of complexity).
- **Team tokens** — Approve and Reject vote tokens (1 pair per player).
- **Quest cards** — Success and Fail cards used during missions (multiple per player).
- **Leader token** — Crown-shaped token indicating the current mission Leader.
- **Vote track markers** — 5 markers for tracking consecutive rejected teams.
- **Round track** — Board or card showing 5 mission rounds with team sizes.
- **Score markers** — Tokens to track mission results (Good/Evil).
- **Lady of the Lake token** — Optional loyalty investigation mechanic.

#### Player-Count Team Composition
--- 5 Players ---
- **Good** — 3 (including Merlin). **Evil** — 2 (including Assassin).
- **Mission sizes** — 2, 3, 2, 3, 3.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Good** — 4. **Evil** — 2.
- **Mission sizes** — 2, 3, 4, 3, 4.

--- 7 Players ---
- **Good** — 4. **Evil** — 3.
- **Mission sizes** — 2, 3, 3, 4*, 4. (*4th mission requires 2 Fails.)

--- 8 Players ---
- **Good** — 5. **Evil** — 3.
- **Mission sizes** — 3, 4, 4, 5*, 5. (*4th mission requires 2 Fails.)

--- 9 Players ---
- **Good** — 6. **Evil** — 3.
- **Mission sizes** — 3, 4, 4, 5*, 5. (*4th mission requires 2 Fails.)

--- 10 Players ---
- **Good** — 6. **Evil** — 4.
- **Mission sizes** — 3, 4, 4, 5*, 5. (*4th mission requires 2 Fails.)

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **Place round track** — Shows 5 mission rounds with team sizes for your player count.
2. **Set vote track** — Place 5 markers in a row to track consecutive rejected teams (5 rejections = Evil auto-wins).
3. **Prepare quest cards** — Give each player 1 Success card and 1 Fail card (Good players will only use Success).
4. **Place team tokens** — Give each player 1 Approve and 1 Reject vote token.
5. **Score area** — Designate a space to track mission results (pass/fail) for each of the 5 rounds.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Role Distribution
1. **Select characters** — Choose which character cards to include based on player count and desired complexity.
2. **Minimum required** — Merlin (Good) and Assassin (Evil) are always included.
3. **Shuffle and deal** — Each player receives one character card face-down and views it secretly.

#### Night Phase (Eyes Closed)
1. **All close eyes** — Everyone extends a fist forward.
2. **Evil reveals** — Minions of Mordred open eyes and identify each other (except Oberon, who keeps eyes closed).
3. **Evil closes eyes** — Minions close eyes.
4. **Merlin sees Evil** — Merlin opens eyes; Evil players extend thumbs so Merlin can identify them (Mordred, if in play, does NOT extend thumb).
5. **Merlin closes eyes** — Merlin closes eyes.
6. **Percival sees Merlin** (if in play) — Percival opens eyes; Merlin and Morgana both extend thumbs (Percival does not know which is real).
7. **All open eyes** — Game begins.

#### Optional Characters
- **Percival** (Good) — Sees Merlin (and Morgana if in play); protects Merlin by creating cover.
- **Morgana** (Evil) — Appears as Merlin to Percival; sows confusion.
- **Oberon** (Evil) — Does not know who other Evil players are; they do not know Oberon.
- **Mordred** (Evil) — Hidden from Merlin; Merlin does not see Mordred as Evil during night phase.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Determining First Leader
- **Random selection** — Choose a starting Leader randomly.
- **Give Leader token** — The starting Leader receives the crown token.
- **Clockwise rotation** — The Leader token passes clockwise after each team vote (approved or rejected).

#### First Mission
1. **Leader proposes team** — The Leader selects players for Mission 1 (team size per round track).
2. **Table votes** — All players vote Approve or Reject simultaneously.
3. **Proceed based on vote** — Majority Approve: mission proceeds. Majority Reject: leadership passes clockwise, vote track advances.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Phase 1: Team Building
1. **Leader proposes** — The current Leader selects a team of the required size for the current mission.
2. **Discussion** — Open discussion about the proposed team. Anyone may argue for or against.
3. **Vote** — All players simultaneously reveal Approve or Reject.
4. **Majority Approve** — Team is locked in; proceed to Phase 2.
5. **Majority Reject (or tie)** — Team is rejected. Advance the vote track by 1. Pass Leader token clockwise. New Leader proposes a new team.

#### Phase 2: Quest
1. **Team members play quest cards** — Each team member secretly selects Success or Fail and places it face-down.
2. **Shuffle and reveal** — Collect all quest cards, shuffle them, then reveal.
3. **Determine outcome** —
   - **All Success** — Mission succeeds (Good point).
   - **One or more Fail** — Mission fails (Evil point).
   - **Exception** — 4th mission with 7+ players requires 2 Fail cards to fail.
4. **Record result** — Place a pass or fail marker on the round track.
5. **Pass Leader** — Leader token passes clockwise for the next mission.

#### Important Rule
- **Good players MUST play Success** — Loyal Servants and Merlin can only play Success cards. They cannot sabotage.
- **Evil players choose** — Minions and the Assassin may play either Success or Fail.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Leader Actions
- **Propose team** — Select exactly the number of players shown for the current mission.
- **Include self** — The Leader may include themselves on the team.
- **Any combination** — There are no restrictions on team composition (same players can go on consecutive missions).

#### Voter Actions
- **Approve** — Support the proposed team.
- **Reject** — Oppose the proposed team.
- **Simultaneous reveal** — Everyone reveals at the same time to prevent bandwagoning.

#### Quest Card Actions
- **Success** — Good players must always play this. Evil players play this to appear trustworthy.
- **Fail** — Only Evil players can play this. Sabotages the mission for their team.

#### Discussion Actions
- **Claim roles** — Players may claim any character (truthfully or falsely).
- **Accuse** — Point out suspicious voting or mission patterns.
- **Analyze** — Track who has been on failed missions and voting patterns.
- **Lie freely** — There are no rules against deception during discussion.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Five Consecutive Rejections
- **Auto-fail** — If 5 consecutive team proposals are rejected (vote track fills), Evil wins automatically.
- **Pressure mechanism** — Forces the table to approve teams even if suspicious, preventing indefinite blocking.
- **Reset** — The vote track resets to 0 after any mission is conducted (pass or fail).

#### Fourth Mission Exception (7+ players)
- **Two Fails required** — The 4th mission requires 2 Fail cards to fail instead of the usual 1.
- **Strategic implication** — Evil needs to get 2 members on the team, or the mission will succeed even with 1 Evil member.

#### Assassin's Final Gambit
- **Trigger** — If Good wins 3 missions, the game does NOT end immediately.
- **Assassination** — The Assassin (with Evil team consultation) gets one chance to identify Merlin.
- **Correct identification** — If the Assassin correctly names Merlin, Evil wins despite losing 3 missions.
- **Incorrect identification** — If the Assassin names the wrong player, Good wins.
- **Core tension** — Merlin knows all Evil players but must be subtle; being too helpful makes you a target.

#### Lady of the Lake (Optional)
- **Loyalty check** — The player holding the Lady of the Lake token may investigate one other player's loyalty.
- **Token passes** — After investigation, the Lady token passes to the investigated player.
- **May lie** — The investigator may report the result honestly or lie.
- **Cannot re-investigate** — Players who previously held the Lady cannot be investigated again.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Good Team Victory
- **3 successful missions** — Good must win 3 of 5 missions AND survive the Assassin's attempt to identify Merlin.

#### Evil Team Victory
- **3 failed missions** — Evil wins immediately upon failing 3 missions.
- **5 rejected teams** — Evil wins if 5 consecutive team proposals are rejected.
- **Assassinate Merlin** — Even after Good wins 3 missions, Evil wins if the Assassin correctly identifies Merlin.

#### No Point Scoring
- **Team win/loss** — The game ends in victory for either Good or Evil.
- **No VP** — There are no victory points or scoring categories.

#### Game End Sequence
1. **Announce trigger** — Declare how the game ended (3rd mission win/fail, 5 rejections, or assassination).
2. **Assassin phase (if applicable)** — Evil team deliberates and Assassin names a player as Merlin.
3. **Reveal roles** — All players reveal their character cards.
4. **Declare winner** — Good or Evil wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Good Team Priorities
- **Vote patterns matter** — Track who approves and rejects which teams; Evil players often approve teams containing Evil members.
- **Mission results analysis** — A failed mission guarantees at least one Evil player was on the team.
- **Protect Merlin** — If you are Merlin, do not be too specific too early; guide the team subtly.
- **Build a trusted core** — Identify 2-3 players you believe are Good and propose teams around them.

#### Evil Team Priorities
- **Split suspicion** — Avoid having all Evil players on the same failed mission; rotate who sabotages.
- **Vote like Good** — Reject teams that include you to look like a cautious Good player.
- **Create doubt** — Accuse trusted Good players to fracture the Good team's coalition.
- **Track Merlin** — Watch for players who seem to know too much too early.

#### Merlin Priorities
- **Feed information indirectly** — Suggest voting against Evil players without stating certainty.
- **Appear analytical** — Frame your knowledge as logical deduction, not definitive knowledge.
- **Distribute suspicion** — Do not focus accusations on one Evil player; spread attention to avoid revealing your perfect information.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Merlin Being Too Obvious
- **Why it fails** — If Merlin clearly identifies all Evil players, the Assassin will easily identify Merlin after 3 Good missions.
- **Fix** — Merlin should appear to learn information gradually, not reveal everything at once.

#### Mistake 2 — Approving Every Team
- **Why it fails** — Letting suboptimal teams go on missions gives Evil easy opportunities to sabotage.
- **Fix** — Reject suspicious teams, but be mindful of the 5-rejection auto-loss.

#### Mistake 3 — Evil Players Always Failing Missions
- **Why it fails** — A player who is on every failed mission is easily identified.
- **Fix** — Evil players should sometimes play Success to build trust, then sabotage at critical moments.

#### Mistake 4 — Ignoring Vote History
- **Why it fails** — Voting patterns contain the most reliable information in the game.
- **Fix** — Track every vote and look for players who consistently approve teams with Evil members.

#### Mistake 5 — Good Players Arguing Publicly About Merlin's Identity
- **Why it fails** — Open discussion about who Merlin might be helps the Assassin.
- **Fix** — Keep Merlin speculation private or vague.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Team Composition
- **Safe team** — Propose players who have been on successful missions.
- **Information team** — Include untested players to learn whether they are Good or Evil.

#### Decision 2 — Evil: Fail or Succeed on This Mission
- **Fail** — Moves Evil closer to winning 3 missions but increases suspicion on team members.
- **Succeed** — Builds trust for future missions but gives Good a point.

#### Decision 3 — Approve or Reject the Proposed Team
- **Approve** — Moves the game forward and prevents the vote track from advancing.
- **Reject** — Blocks a suspicious team but risks the 5-rejection auto-loss.

#### Decision 4 — Assassin: Who Is Merlin?
- **Behavioral analysis** — Target the player who seemed to know the most throughout the game.
- **Process of elimination** — Rule out players who were wrong about Evil identities.
- **Reverse psychology** — Consider players who were notably quiet or cautious, as Merlin often plays this way.
